q1.prompt: A microbiology student is comparing members of the Picornaviridae and Orthomyxoviridae families. She notes that while most picornaviruses (excluding rhinoviruses) can survive transit through the acidic environment of the gastrointestinal tract to be transmitted via the fecal-oral route, orthomyxoviruses are unable to do so. Which structural property of orthomyxoviruses primarily accounts for their susceptibility to inactivation by gastric acid?

options.label: Presence of a lipid-containing envelope
Helical capsid symmetry
Possession of a segmented genome
Use of a viral-encoded RNA-dependent RNA polymerase
Absence of a tegument layer

q2.prompt: A 30-year-old patient presents with symptoms of acute hepatitis. Laboratory serology results are negative for Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg) and negative for Hepatitis B surface antibody (anti-HBs). However, the patient tests positive for IgM antibody to the Hepatitis B core antigen (IgM anti-HBc). Based on these markers, what is the most likely clinical stage of the infection?

options.label: Early acute infection
Chronic carrier state
The "window period"
Complete recovery and immunity
Post-vaccination status

q3.prompt: Influenza A virus is capable of causing major global pandemics through a process known as "antigenic shift," whereas the measles virus (a paramyxovirus) only undergoes minor "antigenic drift." Which genomic characteristic of Influenza A allows for the reassortment of genetic material that leads to this sudden shift?

options.label: Presence of a circular RNA genome
Possession of a segmented RNA genome
Integration into the host cell chromosome
Use of a diploid positive-sense RNA genome
Lack of a viral-encoded RNA polymerase

q4.prompt: During the maturation of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV), the precursor glycoprotein gp160 is cleaved into two functional subunits. Which of these resulting proteins is primarily responsible for the initial attachment of the virion to the host CD4 receptor?

options.label: gp41
gp120
Reverse transcriptase

q5.prompt: While most DNA viruses utilize the host cell's nucleus for their replication and transcription, members of the Poxviridae family are unique in their ability to replicate entirely within the host cell's cytoplasm. What is the primary biochemical reason for this independence from the host nucleus?

options.label: They possess a linear single-stranded DNA genome
They carry their own viral DNA-dependent RNA polymerase
They utilize a reverse transcriptase step in the mitochondria
They replicate only in the host cell's nucleolus
They utilize host cell ribosomes directly to translate DNA

q6.prompt: Acyclovir is clinically effective for treating Herpes Simplex Virus and Varicella-Zoster Virus infections, but it typically lacks clinical activity against Cytomegalovirus. Which biochemical factor explains why CMV is naturally resistant to the effects of acyclovir?

options.label: CMV lacks a viral-encoded DNA polymerase
CMV does not produce the specific thymidine kinase needed to phosphorylate the drug
Acyclovir cannot penetrate the large, complex CMV envelope
CMV replicates exclusively in the host cell cytoplasm
CMV utilizes a protein-based primer for DNA synthesis

q7.prompt: Neuraminidase inhibitors, such as oseltamivir and zanamivir, are used for the treatment and prophylaxis of Influenza A and B. By inhibiting the viral neuraminidase enzyme, these drugs specifically block which stage of the viral life cycle?

options.label: Uncoating of the viral genome via M2 channels
Attachment to host cell sialic acid receptors
Release of mature progeny virions from the host cell
Transcription of negative-strand RNA into mRNA
Proteolytic processing of viral polyproteins

q8.prompt: Rhinovirus is the most common cause of the "common cold" but, unlike other members of the Picornaviridae family such as Poliovirus or Hepatitis A, it does not cause gastrointestinal disease. Which property of Rhinovirus accounts for its inability to establish an infection in the digestive tract?

options.label: It possesses a helical rather than icosahedral capsid
It posses a ssRNA genome
It is an enveloped virus
It requires a coreceptor found only in the lung
It replicates optimally only at 37°C

q9.prompt: A 34-year-old HIV-positive patient is initiated on a regimen containing zidovudine (AZT). The healthcare team plans to monitor the patient's complete blood count regularly to assess for toxicity. Which dose-limiting adverse effect of this medication are they primarily monitoring for?

options.label: Reversible sensory peripheral neuropathy
Acute pancreatitis and lactic acidosis
Myelosuppression resulting in anemia
Obstructive crystalline nephropathy
Stevens-Johnson syndrome

q10.prompt: Researchers attempting to develop a vaccine for the Hepatitis C virus have faced significant challenges due to the virus's rapid genetic evolution and high degree of antigenic variation. Which biochemical characteristic of the HCV replication cycle is responsible for this genetic instability?

options.label: Integration of the viral genome into host chromosomes as a provirus
High error rate of the viral-encoded reverse transcriptase
Lack of 3'-5' exonuclease proofreading activity in the RNA polymerase
Requirement of a helper virus for successful viral assembly
Formation of circular partially double-stranded DNA intermediates
